lines.en_lit: And if extended it is extended for the seller, well goat, bull, what is there to say? There is a decree bull due to the goat.

due to a decree should die And furthermore, sin-offering whose year has passed goes to graze. As Reish Lakish said: We consider a sin-offering whose year has passed as though it stands in a cemetery, it grazes
Rather, Rava said: decree due to mishap One may neither consecrate nor valuate, nor dedicate for sacred use at this time,

And if one did consecrate, valuate, or dedicate an animal it is uprooted, produce, garments, or vessels they decompose. money or metal vessels, he transport benefit to the Dead Sea. And what uprooting? He locks the door before it, and it dies on its own
What mishap If mishap of offering even all graze. If mishap of shearing and working even all graze.
Actually, a mishap of sacrifice. And those which are not fit for sacrifice, he is not preoccupied with, This which are fit for sacrifice he is preoccupied with

itself, mishap, is tanna’im. As it was taught one A Paschal lamb that was not sacrificed on the first sacrificed on the second on the second it is sacrificed the following year. And it was taught another it is not sacrificed What, is it not that they disagree about mishap?
No, that everyone agrees we are not concerned about a mishap, and here they disagree about the dispute between Rabbi and the Rabbis And not difficult. This Rabbi That the Rabbis,
Wasn’t it taught And, so too, money Rather, isn’t it conclude from this that they disagree with regard to a mishap? learn from this

MISHNA: comes over to the scapegoat, places both his hands upon it, and confesses. And he would say as follows: Please, God, Your people, the house of Israel, have sinned, and done wrong, and rebelled before You. Please, God, grant atonement, please, for the sins, and for the wrongs, and for the rebellions that they have sinned, and done wrong, and rebelled before You, Your people, the house of Israel, as it is written in the Torah of Moses Your servant, saying: “For on this day atonement shall be made for you to cleanse you of all your sins; before the Lord you shall be purified”

And the priests and the people standing in the courtyard, when they would hear the Explicit Name emerging from the mouth of the High Priest, they would kneel and prostrate and fall on their faces, and say: Blessed is the name of His glorious kingdom forever and ever. passed to the one who was to lead it everyone is eligible to lead it, but the High Priests established fixed and did not allow an Israelite to lead it.
Rabbi Yosei said: an incident where Arsela led and he was an Israelite. And they made a ramp for due to the Babylonian who would pluck at hair and say to Take and go, take and go,

GEMARA: whereas he did not say: The children of Aaron, Your sacred people, Who taught Rabbi Yirmeya said: is not in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda, for if in accordance with Rabbi Yehuda, didn’t say: atonement through the scapegoat,
Abaye said: Even you say Rabbi Yehuda, is that to say that priests are not included among: Your people, Israel?
passed to the one who was to lead it The Sages taught, man to qualify a non-priest appointed
